description_long: This sculpture depicts a stylized human figure carved from a single block of stone, standing upright with both arms bent at the elbows and hands resting at the waist. The figure wears a knee-length garment tied with a belt, and its facial features are roughly defined, with prominent eyes, nose, and lips. The head is adorned with elaborate, symmetrical headdress elements or hair, and the back shows two thick, rope-like strands hanging down. The surface is weathered and textured, suggesting age and exposure to the elements. The base is irregular and appears to be part of the same stone as the figure, providing stability. The overall form is blocky and robust, with a sense of monumentality typical of ancient or traditional public sculptures.
description: A weathered stone sculpture of a human figure with a detailed headdress and a belted garment, standing upright on a rough base. The carving is robust and textured, with stylized facial features and prominent arms.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full face, headdress, arms bent at the waist, and the tied garment.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the profile of the face, the depth of the headdress, and the arm's projection.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the headdress and two thick, rope-like strands, as well as the plain back of the garment.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the profile of the face, the arm, and the side of the garment and base.
attribute_location_context: The sculpture appears to be displayed on a flat surface, possibly in an outdoor or semi-outdoor public space, with no visible architectural context.
